license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.yxefxje
title: Vandy - Hyperglycemic clamp
author: Li Kang

steps_raw.instruction: Surgical catheterization of the carotid artery and jugular vein in mice at least 5 days prior to the day of the study (refer to protocol for Surgical Catheterization of the Carotid Artery and Jugular Vein).
Weigh mouse and start fast (suggested starting time between 7:00 and 8:00 AM) by placing mouse in a plastic container with fresh bedding.

Mouse is hooked up to the swivel 4 hours into fasting and basal blood/plasma samples are collected 15 and 5 mins before the start of the clamp (refer to protocol for Hyperinsulinemic-Euglycemic Clamp for detailed set-up and connections).
After a total of 5 hr fast, variable infusion of 50% glucose starts. Arterial glucose is increased and maintained at 250-300 mg/dL.
Donor blood is infused to jugular vein catheter throughout the study to prevent a fall of hematocrit.
Plasma insulin and C-Peptide concentrations are measured at times as described in the following study sheet.

At the end of the study, mouse is anesthetized and tissues of interest are harvested and frozen in liquid nitrogen. I: sample for plasma insulin concentration (25 µl plasma) C: sample for C-peptide at t = -5,15,100, and 120 (50 µl blood)
